characters.name: leo
characters.scene: Full-body single-character HERO key art of Léo Macedo, the 100% human 12-year-old Brazilian boy who lives entirely in the OUTSIDE real world. He stands in a clean, grounded silhouette of a lanky, slightly tall-for-his-age 12-year-old Brazilian pardo boy in a natural, observant, and relatable tween pose, holding his worn school backpack close over one shoulder with a city-mapping sketchbook in hand. His design is 100% human living in the outside real world—NEVER inside Córtex City, zero bioluminescence, no sci-fi armor, no portal or brain-entry imagery, and never a generic heroic child model. He is an authentic Brazilian tween with curly/kinky hair that he casually pats down, intelligent observant eyes, and an expression of everyday vulnerability and curiosity. He wears a simple, modest working-middle-class school uniform—a plain short-sleeve Colégio Santa Clara t-shirt without readable words or crests and casual shorts—along with well-worn sneakers, carrying a battered backpack and a notebook filled with hand-drawn city maps and street sketches. Set against a soft, secondary painterly backdrop of ordinary Brazilian daylight and everyday Copacabana urban texture—a modest apartment hallway with vintage ceramic floor tiles, an old iron elevator grille, or a quiet school corridor bathed in warm, natural tropical daylight and washed earthy tones. Absolutely no tourist postcard landmarks like Cristo Redentor or Sugarloaf, and no favela stereotypes.
